changes.motivation: bun validates all workspaces listed in the root package.json exist at install time, even when using --filter to restrict which package gets installed. The Containerfile was copying package.json stubs for packages/cli and packages/scraper but missing packages/website, causing the build to fail with 'Workspace not found packages/website'.
changes.summary: Added COPY packages/website/package.json packages/website/package.json to packages/scraper/Containerfile so bun can resolve all three workspace members declared in the root package.json before running bun install --filter scrape --production.

problems: The Containerfile comment explicitly documents the pattern of copying package.json stubs for workspace resolution, but packages/website was omitted. The comment should have listed all three workspaces or referenced the root package.json workspaces array to make it obvious all members need stubs.

Bun's behavior of validating all workspaces even when --filter is used is not obvious from the CLI documentation. This caused a non-intuitive failure: the filter restricts installation to one package, but all workspace members must still have resolvable package.json files on disk.
